question: Promote meaningful knowledge from local Codex session history into DinoBrain accepted memory without syncing raw transcripts: extract repeated user preferences, important decisions, mistakes, setup/hook/search lessons, and future behavior rules from the metadata-only session registry and local Codex sessions; create reviewed safe memory artifacts and verify retrieval/public-data safety.

items.title: Behavior rule: In similar future work, apply this decision: Define true compounding as repeated conversion of conversations into reviewed preferences, decisions, claims, corrections, and behavior rules that measurably improve future sessions.

Added verify:compounding to prove later memory search/context packs retrieve the promoted rule and memory-on behavior beats memory-off baseline.

items.title: Behavior rule: In similar future work, apply this decision: Register sessions first as metadata-only local-only source archives plus a searchable Wiki registry.

items.title: Behavior rule: In similar future work, apply this decision: Verified the installed/current DinoBrain OS loop against the user's 8-step flow. Codex MCP registration, UserPromptSubmit hook registration, hook simulation/probe, task/context pack creation, wiki_search, finish_task support, v2 verification, and git remotes are present. The complete loop is partially rather than fully autonomous: live current-thread hook injection was not observed before manual probe, model behavior cannot be runtime-enforced by DinoBrain, search_memory is not a separate tool, knowledge growth beyond accepted instances is not fully automatic, and git_sync remains dry-run/manual-review by policy.

items.title: Behavior rule: In similar future work, apply this decision: Implemented a post-install Codex hook approval flow. The installer now creates DinoBrain Codex Hook Approval.cmd and launches it after hook registration unless skipped. The helper detects stale Codex desktop processes, restarts/reopens Codex, copies /hooks to the clipboard, and shows instructions while preserving the requirement that the user manually trusts the hook. Updated the Windows setup UI with a checked-by-default approval flow option, added verification, updated uninstall cleanup, bumped version to 0.1.4, pushed main/tag, uploaded the v0.1.4 release ZIP, and verified the uploaded private asset hash.

items.title: Behavior rule: In similar future work, apply this decision: Explained that Codex hook trust approval should not be automated because it is the security boundary for command-running hooks. Verified local DinoBrain docs/code state: installer can register user-level hooks, enable hook runtime, run an installed-hook handshake, and provide diagnostics, but cannot bypass Codex's hook trust prompt.

items.title: Behavior rule: In similar future work, apply this decision: Fixed installer Codex UserPromptSubmit registration so DinoBrain is merged into the first existing prompt-hook group instead of being appended as a separate second group. Only install.ps1 and scripts/verify-installer-hooks.ps1 were changed. Added regression cases preserving why-was-fable-banned and verifying DinoBrain appears exactly once in the first UserPromptSubmit group. Created branch fix/codex-userprompt-hook-merge, committed, pushed, and opened PR #1.
